MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PLAINTIFFS ANTHONY FOURDYCE, DIANE FOURDYCE, and THE ESTATE OF KELSEY C. FOURDYCE's Motion For An Order Establishing Admissions; Request For Terminating Sanctions.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Motion to deem requests for admissions is GRANTED. CCP 2033.280. No timely opposition filed. The request for terminating sanctions is also GRANTED. By deeming admitted the requests for admission, there is essentially nothing left to litigate. Moreover, there is a long history of discovery abuse, including a failure to comply with discovery or court orders, and there is no evidence that would show less severe sanctions would produce compliance with discovery. See Moofly Productions LLC v. Favila (2020) 46 Cal.App.5th 1, 10-12 (collecting cases). Defendant Harris Phillip Rosenbloom's answer is STRICKEN. CCP 2023.030(d)(1). The Clerk is directed to default as to Defendant Harris Phillip Rosenbloom. I decline to enter default judgment at this point. Rather, Plaintiffs may file a motion for default judgment once the default is entered. This is to provide Defendant a final opportunity to avoid default judgment.

The prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem at sborrowman@gmail.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Scott Borrowman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/JPT)
